title: Mortgages for Sports Professionals
metaTitle: Athlete Mortgages — Income Recognition for Pros
metaDescription: Mortgage programs for professional athletes with multi-year contracts, signing bonuses, and cross-border income. Specialty lender desks for sports professionals.
intro: Professional athletes face a unique set of mortgage challenges — multi-year contracts with variable annual values, signing bonuses, cross-border tax treatment, and income that may concentrate in a 5–10 year window.

body.heading: What complicates a pro athlete's mortgage file
paragraphs: Standard income recognition struggles with three things: contracts that span multiple jurisdictions and tax years, signing bonuses paid as lump sums (which lenders sometimes refuse to count), and earnings concentrated in a short career window where future income isn't guaranteed.

Specialty desks at a handful of Canadian lenders evaluate athlete income holistically — averaging contract value over the term, recognizing signing bonuses on a pro-rated basis, and considering net worth and savings discipline. The brokerage relationship matters here: I have direct access to underwriters who handle these files routinely.

body.heading: Common scenarios
paragraphs: First contract / rookie: usually requires a parent guarantor or co-signor and equity-based qualification. Mid-career with 3+ years of stable T1 returns: standard or BFS programs work well. End-of-career planning: structure to lock in low fixed payments before retirement and use accumulated savings as down payment on a long-term home.

whoItsFor: Are a professional athlete with a multi-year contract
Have signing bonus or salary structures that don't fit standard underwriting
Are planning the transition from active career to retirement

faqs.question: Can a US-based contract qualify me for a Canadian mortgage?
faqs.answer: Yes — with cross-border tax filings and contract documentation, several Canadian lenders will evaluate USD-denominated contracts. The currency conversion and stability of the contract drive the qualifying income.

faqs.question: How are signing bonuses treated?
faqs.answer: Lender-dependent. Some count them at 50% pro-rated over the contract term. Others ignore them. The right lender matters more than the headline rate for athlete files.
